symbols.name: ANCIENT_RTP
symbols.detail: [DEAD] Exported constant with zero runtime importers and no type-only importers | [UNIQUE] Simple numeric constant. No similar constants found in RAG results. | [OK] Value 0.95 is consistent with standard 95% RTP target. Cannot verify against actual reel weights from this file alone, but no in-file contradiction detected. | [LEAN] Single numeric constant representing the configured RTP. No abstraction at all.

symbols.name: PAY_TABLE
symbols.detail: [USED] Non-exported constant referenced in getPayMultiplier function at line 15 | [UNIQUE] Data structure mapping symbols to payout multipliers. No similar lookups found in RAG. | [OK] All six symbol tuples [3-of-a-kind, 4-of-a-kind, 5-of-a-kind] match the authoritative table in .anatoly/docs/04-API-Reference/02-Configuration-Schema.md and .anatoly/docs/02-Architecture/02-Core-Concepts.md exactly. | [LEAN] Plain Record mapping each pay symbol to a fixed-length tuple of three multipliers, exactly matching the documented paytable schema in .anatoly/docs/04-API-Reference/02-Configuration-Schema.md. Minimal and appropriate.

symbols.name: getPayMultiplier
symbols.detail: [USED] Exported function runtime-imported by 2 files: src/engine.ts, src/legacy.ts | [UNIQUE] RAG explicitly reports no similar functions found for this symbol lookup. | [OK] Index mapping is correct: count 3→row[0], 4→row[1], 5→row[2] matches the documented [3-of-a-kind, 4-of-a-kind, 5-of-a-kind] tuple layout. WILD and SCATTER lack a PAY_TABLE row so !row correctly returns 0, consistent with documented behaviour. | [LEAN] Straightforward O(1) table lookup with three explicit branches for counts 3/4/5. The explicit if-chain is readable and maps directly to the domain. Using row[count-3] would be marginally shorter but the current form is not overengineered.

symbols.name: lineWins
symbols.detail: [DEAD] Exported function with zero runtime importers and no type-only importers | [DUPLICATE] Identical algorithm to checkLine: both find leading symbol, skip WILD, count consecutive matches, return symbol+count if >= 3. Variable names differ (first vs lead, count vs run, symbol vs sym) but semantic logic is 100% interchangeable. | [OK] Leading-WILD resolution, consecutive-run counting, SCATTER/all-WILD early-null returns, and minimum count=3 guard are all logically correct for a left-to-right consecutive payline evaluator. WILD substitution mid-run is handled correctly by the unified loop condition. | [LEAN] Single-responsibility function: resolves the effective lead symbol (accounting for leading WILDs), counts the left-to-right consecutive match, and returns the result or null. Logic is compact and maps directly to documented payline evaluation semantics.

duplicate_target.similarity: Semantically identical — both count consecutive matching symbols allowing WILD wildcards, return null if fewer than 3 matches, differing only in variable/field naming conventions

file_level.general_notes: Best practices score: 8/10 (0 FAIL, 4 WARN, 13 PASS)

rules.detail: All function parameters and return types are explicitly annotated (getPayMultiplier: number, lineWins: { symbol: Symbol; count: number } | null). The file follows strict-compatible conventions. tsconfig.json is not provided, but no code patterns indicate strict mode is disabled.
No explicit or implicit `any` usage found. All variables and parameters carry concrete types. Record<string, [number, number, number]> uses a broad string key but is not `any`.
No type assertions (`as`) present. The nullable return `{ symbol: Symbol; count: number } | null` is clean and idiomatic. No unsafe casting observed.
Record<string, [number, number, number]> is correctly used for the paytable lookup map. Appropriate application of a built-in utility type.

PAY_TABLE is declared as const (preventing reassignment) but its tuple values are mutable arrays — e.g. PAY_TABLE['CHERRY'][0] = 999 is valid at runtime. The tuples should be readonly. Additionally, `as const satisfies Record<string, readonly [number, number, number]>` would enforce both immutability and type correctness.
No local interface or type alias defined in this file. Symbol is imported from types.ts. Consistent with the broader project convention of importing domain types from a central types module.
No obvious lint violations. Consistent indentation, no unused variables, no unreachable code. The early-return guard pattern in getPayMultiplier and lineWins is clean.

All three public exports (ANCIENT_RTP, getPayMultiplier, lineWins) lack JSDoc comments. The paytable semantics (multiplier applied to lineBet, count range 3–5, WILD/SCATTER exclusions) are non-trivial and warrant inline documentation per project API surface.
No deprecated APIs used. ESM-compatible .js extension in imports, modern for-of loop, nullish coalescing (??) — all idiomatic 2026 TypeScript.
Single type-only import from a sibling module, placed at the top of the file. Correct .js extension for ESM resolution. No side-effect imports or circular dependency risk.
No async code in this file. All functions are pure synchronous lookups with explicit null-guard returns. No error handling needed.

Slot-machine / gambling domain inferred from symbol names (CHERRY, LEMON, BELL, BAR, SEVEN, DIAMOND, WILD, SCATTER) and RTP constant. No RNG usage present in this file — it is a pure lookup table, so the regulated-gaming Math.random() concern does not apply here. No hardcoded secrets, no eval, no command injection. Payout multipliers are integer literals only; no floating-point monetary arithmetic.
Simple O(1) hash lookup in getPayMultiplier; O(n) single-pass scan in lineWins with early break. No N+1 patterns, no unnecessary allocations.
Both exported functions are pure — identical inputs always produce identical outputs with no side effects or hidden state. Highly testable without any mocking infrastructure.

The `satisfies` operator (TS 4.9+, idiomatic in 5.x/2026) would strengthen PAY_TABLE's typing: it would catch missing/extra symbol keys against the Symbol union while preserving literal tuple types for narrowing. Currently the explicit Record<string, ...> annotation widens key inference unnecessarily.

Paytable-specific: PAY_TABLE key is typed as `string` rather than a narrower PaySymbol union (the paying subset of Symbol). This means getPayMultiplier(symbol, count) accepts any Symbol (including WILD/SCATTER) and silently returns 0 via the runtime guard, rather than being rejected at compile time. Per .anatoly/docs/04-API-Reference/02-Configuration-Schema.md, WILD and SCATTER are explicitly non-pay symbols — a type-level PaySymbol = 'CHERRY' | 'LEMON' | 'BELL' | 'BAR' | 'SEVEN' | 'DIAMOND' would encode this invariant statically.

suggestions.description: Make PAY_TABLE tuple values readonly using `as const satisfies` to prevent accidental mutation and leverage TypeScript 5.5+ idioms simultaneously.
Introduce a PaySymbol type alias for the paying-symbol subset to eliminate the loose `string` key on PAY_TABLE and make the non-pay exclusion of WILD/SCATTER a compile-time invariant.
